transcript: Finally found the villagers you've been looking for forever! Now let's get them home! Oh wait. Hello and welcome to OMGCraft. I'm your host, OMGChad. So you finally found villagers. Maybe you plan to make a trading center, or a way to automatically harvest and replant crops. Well, you need to get those villagers back home to your base before you can do anything with them. And what are you gonna do? You could make a big rail system that'll take them from one to the other. That's a lot of iron. And also, you gotta make sure you're placing blocks, the whole, that's really bad. I have a much easier tip for you that is new to 1.9 that i'm sure you will love so here we are in a little minecraft world i'm in a village already to move some of these villagers but what is my secret ingredient going to be to move these guys around well it will be a boat very simple to craft a boat. This is the new 1.9 boat with the oars. So what you're going to do is you're going to find your villager that you're going to use. I'm going to go ahead and break a few blocks of this wood here. And you're going to put your boat down next to the villager and just like a minecart the villager will auto place himself into the boat now he will not move if you want him to get out you can break the boat just by punching it a few times oh sorry villager didn't mean to do that and if he's not getting in the boat you can move the boat faster or closer to him and you you can also right-click and move the boat using the oars. With the new boats, they actually move pretty fast on land. You can see how quick I'm going here. Woo! Now, this might not seem very fast, but the very cool thing about this, and which makes it way better than rails, is that you don't need to have anything down beforehand so you move these guys all around it's getting a little bit dark so let's make a day again uh now if you come to this situation where you're trying to get up oh god this villager he's too oh he's too big i can't get up a block don't worry uh's an easy solution. Go ahead and shift and you'll leave the boat. Uh, you cannot get up with stairs or anything like that, but the, the easy, the solution is very easy. Go ahead and place down a piston and then a lever either on the piston, actually next to the piston is actually better. In fact, uh, if I would get rid of that and place it onto the block, either of these two blocks that's touching the piston, that'll work a lot better. Get in the boat, rotate it around, move it on top of that piston, reach over and flip that lever, and now we are up a block. The cool thing is that both the piston and the lever are very quick to destroy so you can replace both of these very quickly to move back up a block there we go and we're off we probably wouldn't want to leave our piston there if we were going to go any further now there is one thing that you want to avoid and that is water water makes it very difficult for you to get out of so we're going to go ahead and jump in this water right now and the reason is you move a lot faster which is great but getting out can be difficult because if you place a piston in water it makes it a lot more difficult to power that piston effectively you basically have to put a piston down inside of the water and then dig over to the appropriate block so that you could place a lever on it and then move the boat over the piston flip the lever so that it moves up then get in the boat and then move it's not impossible but it is a lot more difficult if you have a friend with depth strider which is a video which we will be releasing later this week all about depth strider uh it makes it a lot simpler so that you don't have to get up out of the water. So, oh my gosh, this is the simplest way to move villagers in Minecraft. Fun fact, you can also put other types of mobs in the back of boats. So it could be an ironlem a sheep or even a creeper if you wanted to and transport those around as well this is a really neat feature added to 1.9 so get using it I think that it will come in handy whenever I'm playing Minecraft as well if you enjoyed this video make sure you give it ol' thumbs up. Let me know your thoughts about this video in the comments down below. Make sure that you subscribe to the channel, youtube.com slash omgcraft, and I hope you have a fantastic day, and thanks for watching. Bye!
